content.subtitle: Use
rows: We use **so ... that** and **such ... that** to show the result of a situation or action.
Al is **so good** that he was made manager.

content.subtitle: Form
rows: so + adjective + that
Al is **so good** that he was made manager.
so + many/much + noun + that
Al sells **so many cars** that he was made manager.
such + a/an + adjective + noun + that
Al is **such a good worker** that he was made manager.
such + adjective + plural noun + that
Al gets **such good results** that he was made manager.
such + a lot of + noun + that
Al makes **such a lot of money** for the company that he was made manager.
so + adverb + that
Al works **so well** that he was made manager.

content.subtitle: Use
rows: We use **too** to describe the negative effect of having more than necessary.
It's **too cold** to work in the garden.
We use **enough** to describe the effects of having or not having the right amount.
Carol isn't **patient enough** to work as a teacher.

content.subtitle: Form
rows: too + adjective (+ full infinitive)
It's **too cold** to work in the garden.
adjective + enough (+ full infinitive)
Carol isn't **patient enough** to work as a teacher.
too + many/much + noun (+ full infinitive)
Jonty works **too many hours** to have any hobbies.
enough + noun (+ full infinitive)
We don't have **enough money** to pay our bills.
too + adverb (+ full infinitive)
I got there **too late** to see the manager.
adverb + enough (+ full infinitive)
Jack did the work **quickly enough** to finish half an hour early.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **Too** does not mean the same as **very**. We only use **too** when describing something negative.
**Enough** always comes after the adjective: You're not old enough to work here.
Both **too** and **enough** can be followed by **for**: It's too hot for me in this office.

sections.title: A: Complete with so or such
sections.instructions: Write so or such in each gap.

items.sentence: It was ...... a difficult exam that many students failed.
The film was ...... interesting that I watched it twice.
They had ...... good seats that they could see everything clearly.
She speaks English ...... well that people think she is bilingual.
There was ...... much traffic that we arrived late.
He is ...... a friendly person that everyone likes him.
We had ...... little time that we took a taxi.
It was ...... a lot of homework that I went to bed at midnight.

sections.title: B: Complete with too or enough
sections.instructions: Use too or enough in each sentence.

items.sentence: The soup is ...... hot to eat right now.
I'm not tall ...... to reach that shelf.
We don't have ...... chairs for everyone.
He drives ...... fast, and that is dangerous.
She wasn't speaking clearly ...... for me to understand.
There is ...... much noise in this classroom.
Is the water warm ...... for a swim?
I arrived ...... late to catch the beginning of the lesson.
It was **so / such** cold that we stayed at home.
They are **too / enough** young to drive a car.
We had **so / such** a great time at the concert.
The instructions were clear **enough / too** to follow.
He spent **so much / such many** money on clothes.
It was **such / so** an easy question that everyone answered it.
You're speaking **too / enough** quietly; I can't hear you.
She is patient **too / enough** to work with children.

sections.title: D: Rewrite with so/such/too/enough
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
The box was very heavy, so I couldn't carry it. **TOO**
The box ...... carry.

She is very intelligent, so everyone asks her for help. **SO**
She is ...... everyone asks her for help.

The classroom is not big enough for thirty students. **TOO**
The classroom ...... for thirty students.

It was a very boring book, so I didn't finish it. **SUCH**
It was ...... I didn't finish it.

He doesn't run fast enough to win races. **TOO**
He runs ...... races.

There were many people, so we couldn't find seats. **SO**
There were ...... we couldn't find seats.

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: It was so a nice day that we went to the beach.
items.answer: It was such a nice day that we went to the beach.

items.sentence: She is enough old to travel alone.
items.answer: She is old enough to travel alone.

items.sentence: There are such many cars on this road.
items.answer: There are so many cars on this road.

items.sentence: I'm too tired enough to study tonight.
items.answer: I'm too tired to study tonight.

items.sentence: He speaks English enough well to work as a guide.
items.answer: He speaks English well enough to work as a guide.

items.sentence: We haven't got money enough for the tickets.
items.answer: We haven't got enough money for the tickets.

items.sentence: The bag is ...... heavy for me to carry.
It was ...... good weather that we went for a picnic.
I didn't sleep ...... last night, so I'm tired now.
The test was ...... difficult that half the class failed.
She arrived ...... late to join the meeting.
We had ...... little time that we skipped lunch.

sections.title: G: Mixed revision
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with one suitable word.

items.sentence: The room was ...... noisy that I couldn't concentrate.
It was ...... an exciting game that everyone stood up.
I don't have ...... time to finish this now.
You're driving ...... fast — please slow down.
She is experienced ...... to manage the project.
There were ...... many messages that I couldn't read them all.
The film was ...... boring that we left early.
He had ...... a lot of work that he stayed at the office late.
